option.name: projectFiles
option.value: $PROJECT_DIR$/config/__init__.py;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/config/dbconfig.py;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/dto/RealTimeStockData.py;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/dto/StockBasic.py;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/dto/StockDataDay.py;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/filter/ContinuousRedFilter.py;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/filter/FluctuationRangeFilter.py;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/filter/IntervalRangeFilter.py;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/filter/NewHighFilter.py;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/filter/OneFilter.py;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/filter/TurnoverRateFilter.py;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/interface/Shape.py;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/monitor/config/__init__.py;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/monitor/config/settings.py;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/monitor/models/__init__.py;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/monitor/models/stock_data.py;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/monitor/services/__init__.py;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/monitor/services/alert_checker.py;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/monitor/services/alert_sender.py;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/monitor/services/data_fetcher.py;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/monitor/services/manual.json;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/monitor/services/stock_monitor.py;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/monitor/strategy/__init__.py;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/monitor/strategy/upper_shadow.py;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/monitor/__init__.py;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/monitor/app.py;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/monitor/monitor_stocks.json;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/pattern/FindShrinkage.py;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/pattern/RedStar.py;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/pattern/ShirnkageAfter.py;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/pattern/ShrinkageByDate.py;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/pattern/TestShape.py;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/trade_schedule/AppendMarketData.py;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/trade_schedule/avg_listen.py;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/utils/__init__.py;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/utils/comminUtils.py;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/utils/date_utils.py;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/utils/GetStockData.py;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/utils/IndicatorCalculation.py;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/utils/send_dingding.py;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/utils/send_email.py;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/utils/TradingDayCalculator.py;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/utils/tushare_utils.py;C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v/app.py

option.name: fileSummaryMaps
option.value: {"C:/Users/曹威/PycharmProjects/trade_v":"{\"/utils/send_dingding.py\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following functions:\\n\\n`send_dingtalk_message`: Sends a message to a DingTalk webhook with a title, stock code, and two stock chart images.\\n\\n`generate_stock_image_url`: Generates a URL for a stock chart image from Sina Finance, given a stock code and an optional chart type.\\n\\nThe file also defines several webhook URLs for different DingTalk groups.\",\"/utils/tushare_utils.py\":\"This file appears to be a Python script that retrieves and analyzes stock data using the Tushare library. It contains the following classes and functions:\\n\\n`IndexAnalysis`: A class that provides methods for retrieving and analyzing stock data.\\n\\n`get_stock_daily`: A static method that retrieves daily stock data for a given stock code and date range.\\n\\n`realtime_quote`: A static method that retrieves real-time stock quotes for a given stock code.\\n\\n`stk_factor`: A static method that retrieves stock factor data (RSI, KDJ) for a given stock code and date.\\n\\n`rt_min`: A static method that retrieves minute-level stock data for a given stock code and time period.\\n\\n`get_ma`: A static method that retrieves moving average data for a given stock code.\\n\\nThe script also includes various imports and utility functions for working with stock data, such as date manipulation and file path management.\",\"/pattern/RedStar.py\":\"This file appears to be a Python script that defines a `RedStar` class and several utility functions for analyzing stock data.\\n\\n`RedStar`: A class that provides a method to validate whether a given stock data day meets certain criteria, such as being a \\\"red star\\\" pattern.\\n\\n`valid`: A static method of the `RedStar` class that takes a `StockDataDay` object and applies various filters to determine if the stock data meets the criteria for a \\\"red star\\\" pattern.\\n\\n`IndexAnalysis.get_stock_daily`: A utility function that retrieves daily stock data for a given stock code and date.\\n\\nThe file also imports several other utility classes and functions, such as `StockDataDay`, `Date_utils`, and various filter classes, which are used in the `valid` method of the `RedStar` class.\",\"/monitor/config/__init__.py\":\"There are no classes or functions in the provided document. The document appears to be an empty XML document, which does not contain any meaningful content.\",\"/monitor/services/alert_sender.py\":\"`AlertSender`: A class that manages the sending of alerts for monitored stocks, with support for cooldown periods and alert history.\\n\\n`send_alert`: Sends alerts for a given stock, considering cooldown periods and maintaining an alert history.\\n`get_stock_name`: Retrieves the name of a stock, first checking the configuration and then the `result_dict`.\\n`get_alert_history`: Returns the history of alerts that have been sent.\\n\\nThis file defines an `AlertSender` class that is responsible for sending alerts for monitored stocks, with support for cooldown periods and maintaining an alert history. The class uses the `send_dingtalk_message` function from the `utils.send_dingding` module to send the alerts, and the `result_dict` from the `utils.GetStockData` module to retrieve stock names.\",\"/filter/OneFilter.py\":\"`OneFilter`: A class that provides a set of filters to validate stock data.\\n\\n`valid`: A static method that checks if the given stock data meets certain criteria, such as the percentage change in the closing price and whether the open, close, high, and low prices are all equal.\\n\\nThis file defines a class `OneFilter` that provides a set of filters to validate stock data. The `valid` static method checks if the given stock data meets certain criteria, such as the percentage change in the closing price and whether the open, close, high, and low prices are all equal.\",\"/monitor/monitor_stocks.json\":\"This file appears to be a JSON object that contains information about various stocks and financial instruments. It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather a set of key-value pairs that describe the properties and thresholds for each instrument.\\n\\nThe file provides the following information:\\n\\n1. `\\\"399006.SZ\\\"`: Describes the \\\"创业板指\\\" (ChiNext Index) with common thresholds for price drops and rises.\\n2. `\\\"000001.SH\\\"`: Describes the \\\"上证指数\\\" (Shanghai Composite Index) with common thresholds for price drops and rises.\\n3. `\\\"601933.SH\\\"`: Describes the \\\"永辉超市\\\" (Yonghui Superstores) stock with various thresholds for price drops and rises, as well as specific price and change thresholds.\\n4. `\\\"600111.SH\\\"`, `\\\"000831.SZ\\\"`, `\\\"601012.SH\\\"`: Describe the stocks of \\\"北方稀土\\\" (Northern Rare Earth), \\\"中国稀土\\\" (China Rare Earth), and \\\"隆基绿能\\\" (Longi Green Energy), respectively.\\n5. `\\\"513090.SH\\\"`, `\\\"513780.SH\\\"`, `\\\"513000.SH\\\"`, `\\\"513730.SH\\\"`, `\\\"159742.SZ\\\"`, `\\\"600104.SH\\\"`: Describe various other financial instruments, some of which are marked as \\\"common\\\" or have \\\"ma\\\" (moving average) properties.\\n6. The file also includes a global `\\\"price_thresholds\\\"` section that applies to all the instruments.\\n\\nOverall, this file appears to be a configuration or settings file that defines various thresholds and properties for different stocks and financial instruments, likely used in some kind of trading or investment application.\",\"/app.py\":\"This file is a Flask application that uses the APScheduler library to schedule two tasks: `find_shrinkage_route` and `find_bottom_line_route`. The application also defines two routes, `/find_shrinkage` and `/shirnkage_after`, which call the corresponding functions. The file does not contain any classes, but it does define several functions:\\n\\n`create_app()`: Creates a Flask application and sets up the scheduling tasks.\\n\\n`find_bottom_line_route()`: Handles the `/find_bottom_line` route and calls the `find_bottom_line()` function.\\n\\n`find_shrinkage_route()`: Handles the `/find_shrinkage` route and calls the `find_shrinkage()` function.\\n\\n`find_shrinkage_after_route()`: Handles the `/shirnkage_after` route and calls the `find_shirnkage_after()` function.\\n\\n`schedule_append_market_data()`: A scheduled task that calls the `AppendMarketData.append_market_data()` function.\\n\\nThe file also initializes a global `BackgroundScheduler` object and defines two global sets, `stored_new_high_codes` and `stored_shrinkage_codes`.\",\"/monitor/strategy/__init__.py\":\"There are no classes or functions in the provided document. The document appears to be an empty XML document, which does not contain any meaningful content.\",\"/monitor/services/__init__.py\":\"There are no classes or functions in the provided file. The file appears to be an empty XML document, which is a markup language used to structure and organize data. Without any content, the file does not perform any specific functionality.\",\"/monitor/services/stock_monitor.py\":\"`StockMonitor`: A class that monitors stock prices and sends alerts based on predefined conditions.\\n\\n`start_monitoring`: The main function that continuously fetches real-time stock data, checks for alerts, and sends notifications if any conditions are met.\\n\\nThis file defines a `StockMonitor` class that is responsible for monitoring stock prices and sending alerts when certain conditions are met. The class takes in various components (data fetcher, alert checker, alert sender, and stock data) and coordinates their interactions to perform the monitoring task. The `start_monitoring` function is the main loop that runs continuously, fetching real-time data, checking for alerts, and sending notifications as needed.\",\"/utils/send_email.py\":\"This file is a Python script that sends an email using the SMTP protocol. It connects to the 163 email server, logs in with the provided email account and password, and sends an email with a subject and body to the specified recipients.\\n\\n`MIMEText`: A class that represents the body of an email message.\\n\\n`MIMEMultipart`: A class that represents a multipart email message, which can include both text and attachments.\\n\\n`send_message`: A function that takes a list of email addresses as input and sends the email to those recipients.\",\"/monitor/models/stock_data.py\":\"`StockData`: A class that manages the storage and retrieval of stock data, including the ability to update and retrieve historical data.\\n\\n`_add_to_array`: A helper function that adds an item to an array, maintaining a maximum length.\\n`initialize_data_storage`: A method that initializes the data storage for each stock being monitored.\\n`update_data`: A method that updates the stock data storage with new data.\\n`get_stock_data`: A method that retrieves the historical stock data for a given stock.\\n\\nThis file defines a `StockData` class that is responsible for managing the storage and retrieval of stock data. The class provides methods for initializing the data storage, updating the data with new information, and retrieving the historical data for a given stock.\",\"/filter/ContinuousRedFilter.py\":\"This file appears to be a Python script that performs a SQL query to find stock codes that have had three consecutive days of closing higher than opening, and then uses a `ContinuousRedFilter` class to check if a given stock data day is valid based on the results of the SQL query.\\n\\n`StockDataDay`: A class that represents stock data for a single day.\\n\\n`exeQuery`: A function that executes a SQL query and returns the results.\\n\\n`Date_utils`: A utility class for working with dates.\\n\\n`IndexAnalysis`: A utility class for analyzing stock data.\\n\\n`ContinuousRedFilter`: A class that checks if a given stock data day is valid based on the results of the SQL query.\",\"/trade_schedule/avg_listen.py\":\"This file contains the implementation of the `EnhancedStockMonitor` class and several supporting functions. The main purpose of this file is to monitor stock prices, detect when they cross certain moving average thresholds, and send alerts via a Dingtalk messaging service.\\n\\n`EnhancedStockMonitor`: A class that monitors stock prices, detects when they cross certain moving average thresholds, and sends alerts via a Dingtalk messaging service.\\n\\n`_send_dingtalk_alert`: Sends a Dingtalk message with the specified content.\\n`_generate_alert_id`: Generates a unique identifier for a stock price alert.\\n`_check_cooldown`: Checks if a stock price alert is within the configured cooldown period.\\n`generate_alert`: Generates and sends a stock price alert.\\n`get_ma_hierarchy`: Dynamically generates the current moving average hierarchy (from highest to lowest).\\n`check_any_break`: Checks if the stock price has crossed any moving average thresholds.\\n`fetch_ma_data`: Fetches the latest moving average data for all stocks.\\n`process_batch`: Processes a batch of stocks, generating alerts as needed.\\n`run_forever`: Runs the stock monitoring process continuously.\\n`_cleanup_old_alerts`: Cleans up expired stock price alert records.\",\"/filter/IntervalRangeFilter.py\":\"This file appears to be a Python script that performs the following tasks:\\n\\n1. Retrieves the most recent 10 trading dates from a database.\\n2. Calculates the price range (percentage difference between the highest and lowest prices) for each stock over the past 10 trading days.\\n3. Filters out stocks with a price range of 5% or less.\\n4. Defines a class `IntervalRangeFilter` that checks if a given stock\\u0027s daily data meets the criteria for inclusion in the analysis.\\n\\nThe file does not contain any named functions, but it does define the following class:\\n\\n`IntervalRangeFilter`: A class that provides a static method to validate whether a given stock\\u0027s daily data should be included in the analysis based on the stock\\u0027s price range.\",\"/utils/date_utils.py\":\"`Date_utils`: A utility class that provides methods for working with trade calendars and dates.\\n\\n`get_trade_calendar`: Returns the trade calendar for a given year, with caching to improve performance.\\n`get_date_by_step`: Returns the trade date that is `n` steps away from a given date, where `n` can be positive or negative.\\n`get_today`: Returns the current trade date, taking into account whether the current day is a trading day or not.\\n\\nThis file defines a utility class `Date_utils` that provides methods for working with trade calendars and dates. The class uses caching to improve performance and provides functions to retrieve trade dates based on a given date and step.\",\"/utils/TradingDayCalculator.py\":\"`TradingDayCalculator`: A class that calculates the number of trading days between two given dates based on a provided trading calendar.\\n\\n`calculate`: A method that calculates the number of trading days between two given dates, handling date format conversions and boundary conditions.\\n\\nThis file defines a `TradingDayCalculator` class that is used to calculate the number of trading days between two given dates based on a provided trading calendar. The class initializes by loading and processing the trading calendar data, and the `calculate` method is used to perform the actual calculation, handling date format conversions and boundary conditions.\",\"/dto/StockBasic.py\":\"`StockBasic`: A class that represents the basic information of a stock, including its trading code, symbol, name, area, and industry.\\n\\n`get_info()`: A method that returns a dictionary containing the basic information of the stock.\\n\\n`from_dataframe()`: A class method that creates a `StockBasic` instance from a Pandas DataFrame containing the required stock information.\\n\\n`__str__()`: A method that defines the string representation of the `StockBasic` instance.\\n\\nThis file defines a `StockBasic` class that represents the basic information of a stock, including its trading code, symbol, name, area, and industry. The class provides methods to retrieve the stock information and create instances from a Pandas DataFrame.\",\"/pattern/TestShape.py\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following functions:\\n\\n`find_bottom_line`: Finds the stocks with the strongest bottom line and writes their codes to a file, and sends a message to a DingTalk webhook for the top 40 stocks.\\n\\n`find_shrinkage`: Finds the stocks with the strongest shrinkage and writes their codes to a file, and sends a message to a DingTalk webhook for the top 40 stocks.\\n\\n`find_shirnkage_after`: Finds the stocks with the strongest shrinkage after the market close and writes their codes to a file.\\n\\n`find_shirnkage_by_date_after`: Finds the stocks with the strongest shrinkage after the market close based on the date and writes their codes and the shrinkage distance to a file.\\n\\n`find_rsi`: Finds the stocks with RSI less than 30 and prints their codes and RSI values.\\n\\nThe file also imports various modules and utilities, and sets up file paths and stock lists to be used by the functions.\",\"/monitor/services/manual.json\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. It appears to be a JSON document that represents a series of stock market data points for the stock with the ticker symbol \\\"399006.SZ\\\". The data includes the timestamp, open, high, low, close, volume, and previous close price for each 15-minute interval on August 1, 2023.\",\"/utils/comminUtils.py\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following function:\\n\\n`get_uuid()`: This function generates a unique 6-character string based on the host ID, sequence number, and current time using the `uuid.uuid1()` function.\\n\\nThe file is used to generate a unique identifier that can be used in various applications where a unique ID is required.\",\"/monitor/services/data_fetcher.py\":\"`DataFetcher`: A class that fetches real-time stock data from Tushare API or manually from a JSON file.\\n\\n`fetch_realtime_data`: Fetches real-time stock data from Tushare API or returns manually entered data from a JSON file.\\n\\n`get_manual_data`: Reads stock data from a JSON file and returns a list of `StockDataDay` objects.\\n\\nThis file is responsible for fetching real-time stock data from the Tushare API or manually from a JSON file, and returning the data in a format that can be used by other parts of the application.\",\"/utils/IndicatorCalculation.py\":\"`IndicatorCalculation`: A class that calculates the RSI (Relative Strength Index) indicator using the Wilder\\u0027s smoothing method, as used by Tencent\\u0027s Tushare.\\n\\n`calculate_rsi`: A function that calculates the RSI value for a given DataFrame of price data, using the Wilder\\u0027s smoothing method.\\n\\n`get_rsi_values`: A function that calculates the RSI6 and RSI12 values for a given DataFrame of price data, using the `calculate_rsi` function.\",\"/pattern/ShirnkageAfter.py\":\"This file contains several classes and functions related to stock data analysis and filtering.\\n\\n`StockDataDay`: A class that represents daily stock data.\\n\\n`ContinuousRedFilter`: A class that filters stocks based on continuous red days.\\n\\n`FluctuationRangeFilter`: A class that filters stocks based on the fluctuation range.\\n\\n`IntervalRangeFilter`: A class that filters stocks based on the interval range.\\n\\n`TurnoverRateFilter`: A class that filters stocks based on the turnover rate.\\n\\n`Date_utils`: A utility class for working with dates.\\n\\n`ShrinkageByDate`: A class that finds the distance between the current date and the date when a stock\\u0027s volume was the minimum in the last 10 days.\\n\\n`ShirnkageAfter`: A class that performs various filters and calculations on stock data to determine if a stock\\u0027s volume has shrunk compared to its minimum volume in the last 10 days.\\n\\nThe file also includes SQL queries to retrieve and process stock data from a database.\",\"/dto/RealTimeStockData.py\":\"`RealTimeStockData`: A class that represents real-time stock data, including the stock name, trading code, date, time, open price, previous close, current price, high, low, bid, ask, trading volume, and trading amount.\\n\\n`from_dataframe`: A static method that creates a `StockDataDay` object from a pandas DataFrame, assuming the column names match the class attributes.\\n\\nThis file defines a `RealTimeStockData` class that represents real-time stock data, including various attributes such as the stock name, trading code, date, time, open price, previous close, current price, high, low, bid, ask, trading volume, and trading amount. It also includes a static method `from_dataframe` that creates a `StockDataDay` object from a pandas DataFrame, assuming the column names match the class attributes.\",\"/monitor/services/alert_checker.py\":\"This file contains the following classes and functions:\\n\\n`AlertChecker`: A class that checks various conditions to generate alerts for stock prices.\\n\\n`_check_time_window_conditions`: A function that checks the price movement conditions within a specified time window.\\n`_check_price_movement`: A function that checks the price movement within a specified time window and generates alerts based on the thresholds.\\n`_check_price_thresholds`: A function that checks if the current stock price has crossed the configured price thresholds and generates alerts.\\n`_check_ma_breakdown`: A function that checks if the current stock price has broken below the moving averages and generates alerts.\\n`_check_change_thresholds`: A function that checks if the stock price change has crossed the configured change thresholds and generates alerts.\\n`_check_common_by_min`: A function that checks for common trading patterns and indicators based on 1-minute stock data and generates alerts.\\n\\nThe file provides a way to monitor stock prices and generate alerts based on various conditions defined in the configuration.\",\"/config/dbconfig.py\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following functions:\\n\\n`exeQuery`: This function executes a SQL query, retrieves the results, and returns them. It handles connection pooling, reconnection, and resource cleanup.\\n\\nThe file also sets up a global MySQL connection pool and creates a MySQL connection engine using SQLAlchemy. These configurations are used to manage database connections efficiently in a Flask application.\",\"/filter/TurnoverRateFilter.py\":\"This file appears to be a Python script that performs a database query to retrieve stock codes with a high turnover rate, and then uses a filter class to check if a given stock data day meets the criteria.\\n\\n`StockDataDay`: A class that represents stock data for a single day.\\n\\n`TurnoverRateFilter`: A class that provides a static method to validate if a given `StockDataDay` object meets the criteria of having a high turnover rate.\\n\\n`valid`: A static method of the `TurnoverRateFilter` class that checks if a given `StockDataDay` object\\u0027s stock code is in the set of stock codes with a high turnover rate.\",\"/dto/StockDataDay.py\":\"`StockDataDay`: A class that represents daily stock data, including information such as the stock code, trade date, open, high, low, close, and trading volume.\\n\\n`from_daily_dataframe`: A static method that creates a list of `StockDataDay` instances from a pandas DataFrame.\\n\\n`from_json`: A static method that creates a `StockDataDay` instance from a pandas DataFrame row.\\n\\n`from_real_time_dataframe`: A static method that creates a `StockDataDay` instance from a pandas DataFrame with a single row of real-time stock data.\\n\\nThis file defines a `StockDataDay` class and several static methods to create instances of this class from different data sources, such as a daily stock data DataFrame or a real-time stock data DataFrame.\",\"/interface/Shape.py\":\"`Shape`: A base class that defines a common interface for different shapes.\\n\\n`valid()`: A method that should be overridden by subclasses to check the specific features of a shape.\",\"/utils/GetStockData.py\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it performs the following tasks:\\n\\n1. Imports necessary libraries (tushare, pandas, json, and a custom date utility module).\\n2. Initializes the Tushare API with a provided token.\\n3. Retrieves the most recent trade date, basic stock information, and daily market data (close price and total market value) for up to 6000 stocks.\\n4. Merges the stock information and daily data, converts the market value from thousands to millions, and creates a dictionary with the stock code as the key and the corresponding data as the value.\\n5. Handles any exceptions that may occur during the data retrieval process.\",\"/monitor/strategy/upper_shadow.py\":\"There are no classes or functions in the provided document. The document appears to be an empty XML document, which does not contain any meaningful content.\",\"/config/__init__.py\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following function:\\n\\n`settings()`: This function returns `None`.\",\"/monitor/models/__init__.py\":\"There are no classes or functions in the provided document. The document appears to be an empty XML document, which does not contain any meaningful content.\",\"/monitor/app.py\":\"This file is a Flask web application that provides an API for monitoring stock data, checking for alerts, and sending alerts. It also includes functionality for manually triggering detection and reloading the configuration.\\n\\n`Config`: A class that manages the application\\u0027s configuration settings.\\n\\n`StockData`: A class that manages the storage and retrieval of stock data.\\n\\n`DataFetcher`: A class that fetches stock data from external sources.\\n\\n`AlertChecker`: A class that checks for alerts based on the stock data.\\n\\n`AlertSender`: A class that sends alerts to users.\\n\\n`StockMonitor`: A class that coordinates the monitoring of stock data, checking for alerts, and sending alerts.\\n\\n`get_alerts()`: A Flask route that returns the alert history.\\n\\n`manual_input()`: A Flask route that handles manual triggering of the detection process.\\n\\n`reload_config()`: A Flask route that reloads the application\\u0027s configuration.\",\"/filter/NewHighFilter.py\":\"This file appears to be a Python script that implements a filter to identify stocks that have recently reached a new high and have since dropped by a certain percentage. Here\\u0027s a summary of the classes and functions in the file:\\n\\n`StockDataDay`: A class that represents daily stock data.\\n\\n`IndexAnalysis`: A class that provides utility functions for analyzing stock data.\\n\\n`exeQuery`: A function that executes a SQL query and returns the results.\\n\\n`Date_utils`: A class that provides utility functions for working with dates.\\n\\n`NewHighFilter`: A class that implements a filter to identify stocks that have recently reached a new high and have since dropped by a certain percentage.\\n\\n`valid`: A static method of the `NewHighFilter` class that checks if a given `StockDataDay` object meets the criteria for the new high filter.\\n\\nThe main purpose of this file is to implement a filter that can be used to identify potentially undervalued stocks based on their recent price history.\",\"/monitor/config/settings.py\":\"`Config`: A class that manages the configuration settings for the application, including Tushare API token, data collection interval, data retention period, debug mode, alert cooldown, and a list of stocks to monitor.\\n\\n`load_monitor_stocks_config`: A function that loads the stock monitoring configuration from a JSON file, processes the data, and returns the configuration.\\n\\n`reload_config`: A function that reloads the stock monitoring configuration by calling the `load_monitor_stocks_config` function.\\n\\nThis file sets up the configuration for a stock monitoring application, including the ability to load and reload the configuration from a JSON file.\",\"/trade_schedule/AppendMarketData.py\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following functions:\\n\\n`append_market_data()`: This function retrieves market data for all stocks in the `stock_list_filter.csv` file, and appends the data to the `market` table in the database.\\n\\n`append_market_mins_data(ts_code)`: This function retrieves 1-minute and 5-minute market data for the specified stock code, and prints the data.\\n\\nThe file also sets up the necessary configurations and imports, and provides a main entry point to run the `append_market_mins_data()` function.\",\"/monitor/__init__.py\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it is a Python script that retrieves minute-level stock market data from the Tencent Finance website using the `urllib.request` and `json` modules. The script constructs a URL with a random number to fetch the data, converts it to a Pandas DataFrame, and then prints the resulting data. The script also includes comments that suggest exploring other stock codes, indices, and ETFs, as well as handling special cases like delisted stocks, suspended stocks, new IPOs, and stocks with corporate actions.\",\"/filter/FluctuationRangeFilter.py\":\"This file appears to be a Python script that performs a SQL query to find stocks that have not exceeded a 2% daily price fluctuation in the past 20 trading days, and then uses a `FluctuationRangeFilter` class to check if a given stock meets this criteria.\\n\\n`FluctuationRangeFilter`: A class that checks if a given stock meets the criteria of not exceeding a 2% daily price fluctuation in the past 20 trading days.\\n\\n`valid`: A static method of the `FluctuationRangeFilter` class that checks if a given `StockDataDay` object meets the criteria for a valid stock.\",\"/pattern/FindShrinkage.py\":\"This file appears to be a Python script that performs analysis on stock data. It imports various classes and functions from other modules, and defines a `FindShrinkage` class with a `valid` static method. The script also retrieves stock data from a database and performs various filters on the data.\\n\\n`StockDataDay`: A class that represents daily stock data.\\n`Date_utils`: A utility class for working with dates.\\n`db_pool`: A database connection pool.\\n`ContinuousRedFilter`: A filter that checks for continuous red days.\\n`FluctuationRangeFilter`: A filter that checks for fluctuation range.\\n`IntervalRangeFilter`: A filter that checks for interval range.\\n`TurnoverRateFilter`: A filter that checks for turnover rate.\\n`FindShrinkage`: A class that performs shrinkage analysis on stock data.\\n`valid`: A static method in the `FindShrinkage` class that applies various filters to a stock data object and returns a value if the stock meets the criteria.\",\"/pattern/ShrinkageByDate.py\":\"This file contains the following classes and functions:\\n\\n`ShrinkageByDate`: A class that loads stock data from a database and provides a method to find the distance between the target date and the first date where the trading volume is less than the target volume.\\n\\n`_load_data`: A class method that loads the stock data from the database into memory.\\n\\n`find_distance`: A static method that finds the distance between the target date and the first date where the trading volume is less than the target volume.\\n\\nThe file also includes a usage example that demonstrates how to use the `ShrinkageByDate` class to find the distance for a given stock and date.\",\"/utils/__init__.py\":\"There are no classes or functions in the provided document. The document appears to be an empty XML document, which does not contain any meaningful content.\"}"}

option.name: fileSkeletonMaps
option.value:
